____________ test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields[schema-required] _____________

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_validate_rejects_invalid_0')
config_payload = {'version': '1'}, expected_error_parts = ('schema', 'required')

    @pytest.mark.error
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        ("config_payload", "expected_error_parts"),
        [
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1"},
                ("schema", "required"),
                id="schema-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "unknown_schema"},
                ("schema", "unknown_schema"),
                id="schema-unrecognized",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": 1},
                ("schema", "string"),
                id="schema-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": "linux"},
                ("os", "object"),
                id="os-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": {}},
                ("os.name", "required"),
                id="os-name-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": {"name": "windows"}},
                ("os.name", "macos", "linux"),
                id="os-name-invalid",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {"name": "linux", "package_manager": "pacman"},
                },
                ("os.package_manager", "brew", "apt", "yum"),
                id="package-manager-invalid",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "packages": "curl",
                    },
                },
                ("os.packages", "list"),
                id="packages-type",
            ),

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_validate_rejects_invalid_18')
config_payload = {'actions': [{'source': 'file.sh', 'type': 'move'}], 'schema': 'module', 'version': '1'}
expected_error_parts = ('actions[0].type', 'move')

    @pytest.mark.error
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        ("config_payload", "expected_error_parts"),
        [
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1"},
                ("schema", "required"),
                id="schema-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "unknown_schema"},
                ("schema", "unknown_schema"),
                id="schema-unrecognized",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": 1},
                ("schema", "string"),
                id="schema-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": "linux"},
                ("os", "object"),
                id="os-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": {}},
                ("os.name", "required"),
                id="os-name-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": {"name": "windows"}},
                ("os.name", "macos", "linux"),
                id="os-name-invalid",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {"name": "linux", "package_manager": "pacman"},
                },
                ("os.package_manager", "brew", "apt", "yum"),
                id="package-manager-invalid",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "packages": "curl",
                    },
                },
                ("os.packages", "list"),
                id="packages-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "packages": [1],
                    },
                },
                ("os.packages[0]", "string"),
                id="packages-item-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "packages": [""],
                    },
                },
                ("os.packages[0]", "non-empty"),
                id="packages-item-empty",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "applications": "firefox",
                    },
                },
                ("os.applications", "list"),
                id="applications-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "applications": [1],
                    },
                },
                ("os.applications[0]", "string"),
                id="applications-item-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "applications": [""],
                    },
                },
                ("os.applications[0]", "non-empty"),
                id="applications-item-empty",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {"name": "linux", "applications": ["firefox"]},
                },
                ("os.package_manager", "required", "applications"),
                id="package-manager-required-with-apps",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "actions": "copy"},
                ("actions", "list"),
                id="actions-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "actions": [1]},
                ("actions[0]", "object"),
                id="action-item-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"source": "file.sh"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].type", "required"),
                id="action-type-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": 1, "source": "file.sh"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].type", "string"),
                id="action-type-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "move", "source": "file.sh"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].type", "move"),
                id="action-type-unknown",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "actions": [{"type": "run"}]},
                ("actions[0].source", "required"),
                id="action-source-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": 1}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "string"),
                id="action-source-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": ""}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "non-empty"),
                id="action-source-empty",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": "/etc/passwd"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "module-relative"),
                id="action-source-relative",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "link", "source": "a"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "required"),
                id="destination-required-link",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "copy", "source": "a"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "required"),
                id="destination-required-copy",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "copy", "source": "a", "destination": 1}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "string"),
                id="destination-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "hidden": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].hidden", "boolean"),
                id="hidden-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "elevated": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].elevated", "boolean"),
                id="elevated-type",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "git" / "config.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
        assert result.returncode == 1
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)

                ("actions[0].source", "non-empty"),
                id="action-source-empty",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": "/etc/passwd"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "module-relative"),
                id="action-source-relative",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "link", "source": "a"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "required"),
                id="destination-required-link",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "copy", "source": "a"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "required"),
                id="destination-required-copy",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "copy", "source": "a", "destination": 1}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "string"),
                id="destination-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "hidden": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].hidden", "boolean"),
                id="hidden-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "elevated": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].elevated", "boolean"),
                id="elevated-type",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "git" / "config.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
        assert result.returncode == 1
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)

expected_error_parts = ('actions[0].destination', 'required')

    @pytest.mark.error
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        ("config_payload", "expected_error_parts"),
        [
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1"},
                ("schema", "required"),
                id="schema-required",
            ),

                {"version": "1", "schema": "unknown_schema"},
                ("schema", "unknown_schema"),
                id="schema-unrecognized",
            ),

                ("schema", "string"),
                id="schema-type",
            ),

                ("os", "object"),
                id="os-type",
            ),

                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": {}},
                ("os.name", "required"),
                id="os-name-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": {"name": "windows"}},
                ("os.name", "macos", "linux"),
                id="os-name-invalid",
            ),

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_preserves_file_action_order(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        home_dir = tmp_path / "home"
        home_dir.mkdir()
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "shell",
            configs={
                "config.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a.txt",
                            "destination": "~/a.txt",
                        },
                        {"type": "run", "source": "setup.sh"},
                        {
                            "type": "link",
                            "source": "b.txt",
                            "destination": "~/b.txt",
                        },
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={
                "a.txt": "A",
                "b.txt": "B",
                "setup.sh": "echo setup",
            },
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, home_dir=home_dir)
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
        actions = payload["modules"][0]["actions"]
        assert [action["type"] for action in actions] == ["copy", "run", "link"]

                            "defaults write com.apple.dock mru-spaces -bool false"
                        ),
                    },
                    {
                        "name": "No check command",
                        "domain": "com.apple.dock",
                        "key": "autohide",
                        "value": True,
                        "value_type": "bool",
                        "expected_state": "1",
                        "apply_command": (
                            "defaults write com.apple.dock autohide -bool true"
                        ),
                    },
                ]
            ),
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
        actions = _actions_for_module(payload, "system")
        assert [action["type"] for action in actions] == [
            "set_preference",
            "set_preference",
        ]
        assert actions[0]["check_command"] == (
            "defaults read com.apple.dock mru-spaces"
        )
        assert actions[0]["expected_state"] == "0"
        assert "check_command" not in actions[1]
>       assert "expected_state" not in actions[1]

        )

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_orders_preferences_before_dock_without_module_schema(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "system" / "preferences.yaml",
            preferences_schema(
                [
                    {
                        "name": "Pref",
                        "domain": "com.apple.dock",
                        "key": "autohide",
                        "value": True,
                        "value_type": "bool",
                        "apply_command": (

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_validate_rejects_invalid_59')
depends_on = 'core', fragment = 'depends_on'

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        ("depends_on", "fragment"),
        [
            pytest.param("core", "depends_on", id="depends-on-not-list"),
            pytest.param([1], "depends_on[0]", id="depends-on-item-not-string"),
            pytest.param([""], "depends_on[0]", id="depends-on-item-empty"),
            pytest.param([None], "depends_on[0]", id="depends-on-item-null"),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_depends_on(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        depends_on: object,
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects depends_on when it is not a non-empty-string dependency list."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "dev" / "module.yaml",
            {
                "version": "1",
                "schema": "module",
                "depends_on": depends_on,
                "actions": [],
            },
        )
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
>       assert result.returncode == 1

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_file_not_found_before_circular_dep(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Prioritizes file_not_found before checking circular dependencies."""
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "a",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "missing.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/missing.txt",
                        }
                    ],
                    depends_on=["b"],
                )
            },
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "b",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["c"])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "c",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["a"])},
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_profile_dedup_inheritance_chain(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Plan deduplicates inherited modules across transitive profile hierarchy."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "base": {"modules": ["core", "dev-tools"]},
                    "work": {
                        "extends": "base",
                        "modules": ["dev-tools", "work-apps"],
                    },
                    "full": {"extends": "work", "modules": ["core", "media"]},
                }
            ),
        )
        for module_name in ["core", "dev-tools", "work-apps", "media"]:
            write_module(
                tmp_path,
                module_name,
                configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
            )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--profile", "full")
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
>       assert _module_names(payload) == [
            "core",
            "dev-tools",
            "work-apps",
            "media",
        ]

E         
E         Full diff:
E           [
E         -     'core',
E         +     'media',
E               'dev-tools',
E               'work-apps',
E         -     'media',
E         +     'core',
E           ]

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_4.py:1025: AssertionError
___________ test_validate_rejects_bad_extends[extends-empty-string] ____________

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_validate_rejects_bad_exte0')
extends_value = ''

    @pytest.mark.error
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "extends_value",
        [
            pytest.param("", id="extends-empty-string"),
            pytest.param(1, id="extends-not-string"),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_bad_extends(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        extends_value: object,
    ) -> None:
        """Validate rejects empty or non-string extends values in profiles."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "base": {"modules": ["core"]},
                    "child": {"extends": extends_value, "modules": ["core"]},
                }

tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_build_profile_name_collis0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_build_profile_name_collisions_do_not_overwrite_other_profile_outputs(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Build for one profile does not overwrite unrelated profile artifacts."""
        output_dir = tmp_path / "out"
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "a-b": {"modules": ["alpha"]},
                    "a/b": {"modules": ["beta"]},
                }
            ),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "alpha",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "alpha.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/alpha.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"alpha.txt": "alpha"},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "beta",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "beta.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/beta.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"beta.txt": "beta"},
        )
    
        first_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--output",
            "out",
            "--release",
        )
    
        assert first_result.returncode == 0, first_result.stderr
        first_payload = parse_stdout_json(first_result)
        alpha_package = _package_for_profile(first_payload, "a-b")
        alpha_dir = _resolve_reported_path(tmp_path, alpha_package["directory"])
        alpha_archive = _resolve_reported_path(tmp_path, alpha_package["archive"])
        assert (alpha_dir / "alpha" / "alpha.txt").read_text() == "alpha"
        assert not (alpha_dir / "beta" / "beta.txt").exists()
    
        second_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "a/b",
            "--output",
            "out",
            "--release",
        )
    
        assert second_result.returncode == 0, second_result.stderr
        assert (alpha_dir / "alpha" / "alpha.txt").read_text() == "alpha"
        assert not (alpha_dir / "beta" / "beta.txt").exists()
    
        extracted_dir = tmp_path / "alpha-release"
        shutil.unpack_archive(alpha_archive, extracted_dir)
>       assert (extracted_dir / "alpha" / "alpha.txt").read_text() == "alpha"

idx = 0

    def raw_decode(self, s, idx=0):
        """Decode a JSON document from ``s`` (a ``str`` beginning with
        a JSON document) and return a 2-tuple of the Python
        representation and the index in ``s`` where the document ended.
    
        This can be used to decode a JSON document from a string that may
        have extraneous data at the end.
    
        """
        try:
            obj, end = self.scan_once(s, idx)
        except StopIteration as err:

    def raw_decode(self, s, idx=0):
        """Decode a JSON document from ``s`` (a ``str`` beginning with
        a JSON document) and return a 2-tuple of the Python
        representation and the index in ``s`` where the document ended.
    
        This can be used to decode a JSON document from a string that may
        have extraneous data at the end.
    
        """
        try:
            obj, end = self.scan_once(s, idx)
        except StopIteration as err:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        ("file_name", "content"),
        [
            pytest.param("data.json", '{"name":"cfg"}\n', id="json"),
            pytest.param("data.yaml", "name: cfg\n", id="yaml"),
            pytest.param("data.yml", "name: cfg\n", id="yml"),
        ],
    )
    def test_build_copies_referenced_data_files_even_with_config_like_extensions(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        file_name: str,
        content: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Build copies referenced JSON/YAML data files that actions reference."""
        destination = tmp_path / "dest" / file_name
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": file_name,
                            "destination": str(destination),
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={file_name: content},
        )
    
        result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", "macos", "--output", "out"
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        package_dir = _resolve_reported_path(
            tmp_path, parse_stdout_json(result)["packages"][0]["directory"]
        )
        assert (package_dir / "core" / file_name).read_text() == content

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_5.py:1300: AssertionError
_ test_install_script_executes_package_and_app_commands_for_each_manager[brew] _

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_install_script_executes_p0')
monkeypatch = <_pytest.monkeypatch.MonkeyPatch object at 0x746902bb7f80>
os_name = 'macos', package_manager = 'brew', package_name = 'coreutils'
application_name = 'firefox', allowed_prefixes = ('brew ',)

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        (
            "os_name",
            "package_manager",
            "package_name",
            "application_name",
            "allowed_prefixes",
        ),
        [
            pytest.param(
                "macos",
                "brew",
                "coreutils",
                "firefox",
                ("brew ",),
                id="brew",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "apt",
                "curl",
                "vim",
                ("apt ", "apt-get "),
                id="apt",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "yum",
                "wget",
                "htop",
                ("yum ",),
                id="yum",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_install_script_executes_package_and_app_commands_for_each_manager(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
        os_name: str,
        package_manager: str,
        package_name: str,
        application_name: str,
        allowed_prefixes: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        """Installer executes package and application installation via OS manager commands."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "invocations.log"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        for command_name in ["brew", "apt", "apt-get", "yum"]:
            command_path = fake_bin / command_name
            command_path.write_text(
                "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
                f'printf \'%s\\n\' "{command_name} $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            )
            command_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        sudo_cmd = fake_bin / "sudo"
        sudo_cmd.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "sudo $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            'exec "$@"\n'
        )
        sudo_cmd.chmod(0o755)
    
        monkeypatch.setenv("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG", str(log_path))
        monkeypatch.setenv("PATH", f"{fake_bin}:{os.environ['PATH']}")
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    os_name=os_name,
                    package_manager=package_manager,
                    packages=[package_name],
                    applications=[application_name],

_ test_install_script_executes_package_and_app_commands_for_each_manager[apt] __

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_install_script_executes_p1')
monkeypatch = <_pytest.monkeypatch.MonkeyPatch object at 0x746902bb7fb0>
os_name = 'linux', package_manager = 'apt', package_name = 'curl'
application_name = 'vim', allowed_prefixes = ('apt ', 'apt-get ')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        (
            "os_name",
            "package_manager",
            "package_name",
            "application_name",
            "allowed_prefixes",
        ),
        [
            pytest.param(
                "macos",
                "brew",
                "coreutils",
                "firefox",
                ("brew ",),
                id="brew",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "apt",
                "curl",
                "vim",
                ("apt ", "apt-get "),
                id="apt",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "yum",
                "wget",
                "htop",
                ("yum ",),
                id="yum",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_install_script_executes_package_and_app_commands_for_each_manager(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
        os_name: str,
        package_manager: str,
        package_name: str,
        application_name: str,
        allowed_prefixes: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        """Installer executes package and application installation via OS manager commands."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "invocations.log"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        for command_name in ["brew", "apt", "apt-get", "yum"]:
            command_path = fake_bin / command_name
            command_path.write_text(
                "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
                f'printf \'%s\\n\' "{command_name} $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            )
            command_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        sudo_cmd = fake_bin / "sudo"
        sudo_cmd.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "sudo $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            'exec "$@"\n'
        )
        sudo_cmd.chmod(0o755)
    
        monkeypatch.setenv("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG", str(log_path))
        monkeypatch.setenv("PATH", f"{fake_bin}:{os.environ['PATH']}")
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    os_name=os_name,
                    package_manager=package_manager,
                    packages=[package_name],
                    applications=[application_name],
                    actions=[],
                )
            },
        )
    
        build_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", os_name, "--output", "out"
        )
        assert build_result.returncode == 0, build_result.stderr
        package_dir = _resolve_reported_path(
            tmp_path, parse_stdout_json(build_result)["packages"][0]["directory"]
        )
    
        install_result = _run_install(package_dir)
>       assert install_result.returncode == 0, install_result.stderr
E       AssertionError: Traceback (most recent call last):

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_uses_sudo_for_elevated_file_and_run_actions(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Elevated installer actions perform privileged operations under sudo."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "sudo.log"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        sudo_cmd = fake_bin / "sudo"
        sudo_cmd.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "sudo $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            'exec "$@"\n'
        )
        sudo_cmd.chmod(0o755)
    
        destination_dir = tmp_path / "dest"
        run_marker = tmp_path / "ran.txt"
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "dev",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "link",
                            "source": "a.txt",
                            "destination": str(destination_dir / "a.txt"),
                            "elevated": True,
                        },
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "b.txt",
                            "destination": str(destination_dir / "b.txt"),
                            "elevated": True,
                        },
                        {
                            "type": "run",
                            "source": "setup.sh",
                            "elevated": True,
                        },
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={
                "a.txt": "a",
                "b.txt": "b",
                "setup.sh": f"printf ran > '{run_marker}'\n",
            },
        )
    
        monkeypatch_env = {
            "RIG_INVOCATION_LOG": str(log_path),
            "PATH": f"{fake_bin}:{os.environ['PATH']}",
        }
        old_log = os.environ.get("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG")
        old_path = os.environ.get("PATH")
        os.environ.update(monkeypatch_env)
    
        try:
            build_result = run_build(
                entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", "macos", "--output", "out"
            )
            assert build_result.returncode == 0, build_result.stderr
            package_dir = _resolve_reported_path(
                tmp_path,
                parse_stdout_json(build_result)["packages"][0]["directory"],
            )
    
            install_result = _run_install(package_dir)

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_build_profile_resolves_inheritance_and_auto_includes_dependencies(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Profile inheritance resolves ancestor modules before building package modules."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "minimal": {"modules": ["core"]},
                    "work": {"extends": "minimal", "modules": ["app"]},
                }
            ),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "shell",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "app",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[],
                    depends_on=["shell"],
                )
            },
        )
    
        result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "work",
            "--output",
            "out",
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)

E         
E         Full diff:

E         +     'app',

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_executes_runtime_plugin_and_virtual_env_steps_for_pyenv(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Installer executes pyenv runtime plugin and virtualenv creation steps."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "runtime.log"
        pyenv_root = tmp_path / "pyenv-root"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        pyenv_path = fake_bin / "pyenv"
        pyenv_path.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "pyenv $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
        )
        pyenv_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        plugin_path = fake_bin / "pyenv-virtualenv"
        plugin_path.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'plugin_dir="${PYENV_ROOT:-$HOME/.pyenv}/plugins/pyenv-virtualenv"\n'
            'if [[ "${1:-}" == "install" ]]; then\n'
            '  mkdir -p "$plugin_dir"\n'
            "fi\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "pyenv-virtualenv $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
        )
        plugin_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        git_path = fake_bin / "git"
        git_path.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'dest="$3"\n'
            'parent="$(dirname "$dest")"\n'
            'if [[ ! -d "$parent" ]]; then\n'
            '  printf \'%s\\n\' "git-missing-parent $dest" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            "  exit 88\n"
            "fi\n"
            'mkdir -p "$dest"\n'
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "git $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
        )
        git_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "dev",
            configs={

                    [
                        {
                            "language": "python",

                                "name": "pyenv",

                                    {
                                        "name": "pyenv-virtualenv",
                                        "virtual_environments": [
                                            {
                                                "version": "3.11.2",
                                                "name": "default-3.11",
                                            }
                                        ],
                                    }
                                ],
                            },
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
        )
    
        old_log = os.environ.get("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG")
        old_path = os.environ.get("PATH")
        old_pyenv_root = os.environ.get("PYENV_ROOT")

        os.environ["PATH"] = f"{fake_bin}:{os.environ['PATH']}"
        os.environ["PYENV_ROOT"] = str(pyenv_root)

        )

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_build_reports_circular_de0')

    @pytest.mark.error
    def test_build_reports_circular_dependency_before_conflicts(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Circular dependency errors take precedence over destination conflicts."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema({"all": {"modules": ["a", "b", "c", "d"]}}),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "a",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/shared.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"a.txt": "a"},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "b",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "link",
                            "source": "b.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/shared.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"b.txt": "b"},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "c",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["d"])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "d",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["c"])},
        )
    
        result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "all",
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 1

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_execution_copy_works_from_absolute_script_path(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Copy action works when installer is invoked through absolute script path."""
        destination = tmp_path / "dest" / "nested" / "copied.txt"
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "copied.txt",
                            "destination": str(destination),
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"copied.txt": "payload"},
        )
    
        build_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", "macos", "--output", "out"
        )
        assert build_result.returncode == 0, build_result.stderr
        package_dir = _resolve_reported_path(
            tmp_path, parse_stdout_json(build_result)["packages"][0]["directory"]
        )
    
        install_result = _run_install(
            package_dir,
            cwd=tmp_path,
            use_absolute_script_path=True,
        )
